---
title: "Statistical Analysis Plan: [title]"
subtitle: 'DOCUMENT: SAP-yyyy-NNN-XX-v01'
author: '**From:** Felipe Figueiredo **To:** ___'
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_caption: yes
    fig_height: 6
    fig_width: 6
    keep_md: yes
    number_sections: yes
    toc: yes
  pdf_document:
    number_sections: yes
    toc: yes
  word_document:
    fig_caption: yes
    fig_height: 6
    fig_width: 6
    keep_md: yes
    reference_docx: misc/style_SAP_en.docx
    toc: yes
# toc-title: "Sumário"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# ************************************************************
# Template: SAP_OBS_FULL_en.Rmd
# Version:  0.xx.x
# Author:   Felipe Figueiredo
# Created:  2026-03-09
#
# ************************************************************
# QC Date:  YYYY-MM-DD
# ************************************************************

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
options(scipen = 999)
library(pander)
library(knitr)
library(flextable)
library(officer)
library(here)

knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here())
panderOptions('table.style', 'rmarkdown')
panderOptions('table.split.table', 160)

# Initialize counters for Table indices
ind.table.body <- 0
ind.table.app <- 0
```

---

\newpage

**Statistical Analysis Plan: [title]**

**Document version**

```{r history}
Version <- c("01")
Changes <- c("Initial version")
history <- cbind(Version, Changes)
colnames(history) <- c("Version", "Alterations")
# pander(history, split.cells = 70)
kable(history)
```

```{r cleaning, include=FALSE}
# Load cached results (Data prep, data mockup, sample size calculation, etc.)
# This ensures all analysis outputs are available when compiling the report.
# Source 96- will safely check for and load all objects.
source(here("scripts", "96_load_cached_results.R"), encoding = 'UTF-8')
```

---

# Abbreviations

- AIC: Akaike Information Criterion
- AUC: Area Under the Curve
- BMI: Body Mass Index
- CCS: Complete Case Set
- CI: Confidence Interval
- CV: Coefficient of Variation
- d: Cohen's d Effect Size
- DAG: Directed Acyclic Graph
- EDC: Electronic Data Capture
- FAS: Full Analysis Set
- GLM: Generalized Linear Model
- h: Cohen's h Effect Size
- HR: Hazard Ratio
- IAS: Imputed Analysis Set
- ICD: International Classification of Diseases
- IPTW: Inverse Probability of Treatment Weighting
- IQR: Interquartile Range
- IRR: Incidence Rate Ratio
- LMM: Linear Mixed Model
- LogMM: Logistic Mixed Model
- MCAR: Missing Completely at Random
- MAR: Missing at Random
- MNAR: Missing Not at Random
- OR: Odds Ratio
- ROC: Receiver Operating Curve
- RR: Risk Ratio
- SAP: Statistical Analysis Plan
- SAR: Statistical Analytic Report
- SAS: Sensitivity Analysis Set
- SD: Standard Deviation
- SE: Standard Error
- VIF: Variance Inflation Factor

# Introduction

## Context and Rationale

[Analyst Note: Briefly describe the clinical rationale and the motivation behind the research. This section provides the "Why" that guides the "How."]

## Research Questions and Objectives

[Analyst Note: Explicitly state the Primary and Secondary objectives. Ensure they are framed as testable statistical hypotheses.]

# Study Design and Population

## Study Design

[Analyst Note: Define the study architecture (e.g., Cross-sectional, Retrospective Cohort, Case-Control). Reference compliance with relevant guidelines (e.g., STROBE).]

**Recommended reporting guideline**

The EQUATOR network reporting guidelines (<http://www.equator-network.org/>) have seen increasing adoption by scientific journals.
All observational studies are recommended to be reported following the STROBE guideline (von Elm et al, 2014).
<!-- All clinical trials are recommended to be reported following the CONSORT guideline (Schulz K F, Altman D G, Moher D., 2010). -->
<!-- All systematic reviews are recommended to be reported following the PRISMA guideline (Page et al, 2021). -->
<!-- All protocols for systematic reviews are recommended to be reported following the PRISMA-P guideline (Page et al, 2021). -->

<!-- In particular when a retrospective study is conducted using hospital records, it is recommended that the RECORD extension of the STROBE guideline is considered (Benchimol et al, 2015). -->

## Setting and Time period

[Analyst Note: ]

## Censoring Rules

[Analyst Note: ]

## Study Population Definitions

[Analyst Note: Source vs. Target population, Unit of Observation]

## Inclusion and Exclusion Criteria

[Analyst Note: List the specific clinical and demographic filters. This defines the boundaries of the source population.]

# Variable Definitions

## Exposures

[Analyst Note: ]

## Outcomes

[Analyst Note: Define the dependent variables. Use the Zarin (2011) framework below to ensure each outcome is precise enough to be programmed without ambiguity.]

```{r outcomes}
# --- Define Levels for clarity ---
# Level 1: Domain
# Level 2: Specific Measurement
# Level 3: Specific Metric (End value, Change from baseline, Time-to-event)
# Level 4: Method of Aggregation (Mean, Proportion, Odds, Hazard, Incidence Rate)

# --- Internal Registry (Scaffold) ---
# Format: c(Domain, Measurement, Metric, Method of Aggregation)

# Examples
Ex.RR <- c("Example RR", "Mortality", "Hospital Discharge Status", "End value", "Proportion")
Ex.OR <- c("Example OR", "Mortality", "ICD-10 Code", "End value", "Odds")
Ex.HR <- c("Example HR", "Mortality", "ICD-10 Code", "Time-to-event", "Hazard")
Ex.IR <- c("Example IR", "Mortality", "ICD-10 + Duration", "End value", "Incidence Rate")

# Project Outcomes (Fill these in)
P1    <- c("Outcome P1", "Domain", "Measurement", "End value", "Method of Aggregation")
S1    <- c("Outcome S1", "Domain", "Measurement", "End value", "Method of Aggregation")
S2    <- c("Outcome S2", "Domain", "Measurement", "End value", "Method of Aggregation")
S3    <- c("Outcome S3", "Domain", "Measurement", "End value", "Method of Aggregation")
E1    <- c("Outcome E1", "Domain", "Measurement", "End value", "Method of Aggregation")

# --- Combine and Format ---
# Using as.data.frame + t() to ensure row-wise binding with labels
outcomes <- rbind(
  Ex.RR,
  Ex.OR,
  Ex.HR,
  Ex.IR,
  P1,
  S1,
  S2,
  S3,
  E1
) %>% as_tibble(.name_repair = "minimal")

colnames(outcomes) <- c("Outcome", "Domain (L1)", "Measurement (L2)", "Metric (L3)", "Method of Aggregation (L4)")

# --- Render Table ---
# Toggle this block to 'eval: false' or comment out for client delivery
knitr::kable(
  outcomes,
  caption = "Zarin (2011) Outcome Specification Scaffold (Internal Use Only)",
  align = "cllll",
)
```

**Primary outcome**

**Secondary outcomes**

- "Outcome S1"
- "Outcome S2"
- "Outcome S3

**Exploratory outcomes**

- "Outcome E1"

## Covariates

[Analyst Note: ]

## Variable Derivation Rules

[Analyst Note: ]

# Data

## Data Source

[Analyst Note: File names, years, versioning, and acquisition method]

## Data Management

### Data Cleaning and Processing

[Analyst Note: Recoding, class coercions, standardized naming]

### Data Validation

[Analyst Note: Range/logic checks, auditing for "impossible" values]

### Data Flow

[Analyst Note: The journey from raw N to analytic N by applying Section 3.4 Study Population Definitions]

# Statistical methods

## Analysis Sets

### Full Analysis Set (FAS)

[Analyst Note: e.g., all records meeting selection criteria]

### Complete Case Set (CCS)

[Analyst Note: Records with non-missing values for specific variables).

### Imputed Analysis Set (IAS)

[Analyst Note: Optional, as needed per project).

### Sensitivity Analysis Set (SAS)

[Analyst Note: Optional, as needed per project).

## Study Size and Power

[Analyst Note: Justification of available N or power check]

## General Principles

[Analyst Note: Descriptive methods, digit precision, Complex Survey Design logic, time-to-event methods, and reporting of missingness]

The epidemiological profile of the study participants will be described.
Demographic
(sex and age)
and clinical variables
<!-- ( [vars] ) -->
will be described as
mean (SD)
<!-- median (IQR) -->
or as counts and proportions (%), as appropriate.
The distributions of participants' characteristics will be summarized in tables and visualized in exploratory plots.

## Handling of Missing Data

[Analyst Note: Imputation vs. Exclusion rules—the "How-to"]

No missing data imputation will be performed.
All evaluations will be performed as complete case analyses.
Missing data counts and proportions will be reported in tables.

## Analysis of Primary Objective

All comparisons between groups will be performed as univariate analyses.
Continuous variables will be compared between groups with the
independent t test with Welch correction.
<!-- paired t test. -->
<!-- Mann-Whitney test. -->
<!-- signed rank Wilcoxon test. -->
Differences in distribution of categorical variables will be assessed with the
Fisher exact test.
<!-- chi-square test. -->
<!-- McNemar test. -->

<!-- All inferential analyses will be performed in the statistical models (described in the next section). -->

## Analysis of Secondary and Exploratory Objectives

### Analysis of Secondary Objective 1

[Analyst Note: ]

### Analysis of Secondary Objective 2

[Analyst Note: ]

### Analysis of Exploratory Objective 1

[Analyst Note: ]

## Significance and Confidence Intervals

[Analyst Note: Alpha levels, tails]

All analyses will be performed using the significance level of 5%.
All significance hypothesis tests and confidence intervals computed will be
two-tailed.
<!-- left-tailed. -->
<!-- right-tailed. -->

## Statistical Software and Reproducibility

[Analyst Note: R version, package management, `here()` framework]

This analysis will be performed using the statistical software `R` (currently on version `r getRversion()`).

# Limitations and Observations

## Limitations of the Statistical Analysis

[Analyst Note: Model constraints, sample size issues, possible assumption violations]

## Data Quality Observations

[Analyst Note: Anticipated source-specific limitations]

# References

- **SAR-yyyy-NNN-XX-v01** -- [title]
<!-- - Cohen, J. (1988). Statistical power analysis for the behavioral sciences (2nd Ed.). New York: Routledge. -->
- Zarin DA, et al. The ClinicalTrials.gov results database -- update and key issues. N Engl J Med 2011;364:852-60 (<https://doi.org/10.1056/NEJMsa1012065>).
- Gamble C, et al. Guidelines for the Content of Statistical Analysis Plans in Clinical Trials. JAMA. 2017;318(23):2337–2343 (<https://doi.org/10.1001/jama.2017.18556>).
- von Elm E, Altman DG, Egger M, Pocock SJ, Gøtzsche PC, Vandenbroucke JP; STROBE Initiative. The Strengthening the Reporting of Observational Studies in Epidemiology (STROBE) Statement: guidelines for reporting observational studies. Int J Surg. 2014 Dec;12(12):1495-9 (<https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijsu.2014.07.013>).
<!-- - Benchimol EI, Smeeth L, Guttmann A, Harron K, Moher D, Petersen I, Sørensen HT, von Elm E, Langan SM; RECORD Working Committee. The REporting of studies Conducted using Observational Routinely-collected health Data (RECORD) statement. PLoS Med. 2015 Oct 6;12(10):e1001885 (<https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pmed.1001885>). -->
<!-- - Schulz K F, Altman D G, Moher D. CONSORT 2010 Statement: updated guidelines for reporting parallel group randomised trials BMJ 2010; 340 :c332 (<https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj.c332>). -->
<!-- - Page M J, McKenzie J E, Bossuyt P M, Boutron I, Hoffmann T C, Mulrow C D et al. The PRISMA 2020 statement: an updated guideline for reporting systematic reviews BMJ 2021; 372 :n71 (<https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj.n71>). -->
<!-- - Moher D, Shamseer L, Clarke M, Ghersi D, Liberati A, Petticrew M, Shekelle P, Stewart LA. Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis Protocols (PRISMA-P) 2015 statement. Syst Rev. 2015;4(1):1 (<https://doi.org/10.1186/2046-4053-4-1>). -->
<!-- - RMS, 2e; Harrel -->
<!-- - CAR, 3e; Fox -->
<!-- - MASS. 4e; Venables, Ripley -->
<!-- - Baby Rothman, 2e; Rothman -->
<!-- - Modern Epidemiology, 4e; Lash, ..., Rothman -->

# Table Shells

[Analyst Note: ]

# Appendix

This document was elaborated following recommendations on the structure for Statistical Analysis Plans (Gamble, 2017) for better transparency and clarity.

<!-- ## Associated analyses -->

<!-- This analysis is part of a larger project and is supported by other analyses, linked below. -->

<!-- **[assoc_title]** -->

<!-- <[assoc_link]> -->

## Availability

All documents from this consultation were included in the consultant's Portfolio.

<!-- The client has requested that this analysis be kept confidential until a future date, determined by the client. -->
<!-- All documents from this consultation are therefore not published online and only the title and year of the analysis will be included in the consultant's Portfolio. -->
<!-- After the agreed date is reached, the documents will be released. -->

<!-- The client has requested that this analysis be kept confidential. -->
<!-- All documents from this consultation are therefore not published online and only the title and year of the analysis will be included in the consultant's Portfolio. -->

The portfolio is available at:

<https://philsf-biostat.github.io/SAR-yyyy-NNN-XX/>
